title: Transport Layer
text:
Clients MUST send RPC messages to the zrpc plugin using the Zellij pipe mechanism (`zellij pipe`).

The pipe MUST be addressed with the name `zjctl-rpc`.

Clients MUST include the `--plugin` flag specifying the plugin URL.

Clients MUST include the `--plugin-configuration` flag with a session-scoped configuration string of the form `zjctl_session=<SANITIZED_SESSION_NAME>`.

The sanitized session name MUST replace any character outside `[a-zA-Z0-9\-_.]` with `_`.

The request payload MUST be written to the pipe's stdin as a single JSON line.

The payload MUST be followed by a newline character.

The response MUST be read from the pipe's stdout.

The client MUST wait for the pipe process to complete before reading the response.

Only `PipeSource::Cli` messages MUST be handled by the plugin. Messages from other pipe sources MUST be ignored.

Rationale: Zellij pipes are the only officially supported IPC channel between external processes and plugins (per [[ADR-0001]]). The `zjctl-rpc` pipe name provides a stable, collision-free namespace. Session-scoped plugin configuration ensures the plugin instance is tied to a specific session. The sanitization rule prevents shell injection and ensures the configuration string is safe for command-line transport. One-MUST-per-sentence structure ensures each requirement is independently testable.
